Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} at Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}, dating to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and the 41st Emperor {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in shape, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound shape is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with {{ill|Ashlar|ja|切石積み}}, reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor {{ill|Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Tumulus|ja|御廟野古墳}}). However, the tomb was plundered in {{ill|1235|ja|文暦2年}}, during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient Period === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of {{ill|687|ja|持統元年}}, shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November {{ill|688|ja|持統2年}}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|ja|大宝2年}} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|ja|大宝3年}}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern Era === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and this designation remains to this day. == Construction and Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin Burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}} of {{ill|646|ja|大化2年}} restricted the construction of large burial mounds, yet high-ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/040/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Emperor Tenmu] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/041/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Empress Jitō] - Imperial Household Agency [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Octagonal tumuli]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Empress Jitō]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Burial Mound]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Noguchi-ōsaka Burial Mound-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Burial Mound]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Burial Mound]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Noguchi-ōsaka Burial Mound-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Burial Mound]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Burial Mound]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:野口王墓]]

Mound-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Burial Mound]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[ja:野口王墓]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Noguchi Royal Tomb | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|7.52|N|135|48|28.17|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Noguchi Royal Tomb (designated as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum)}} | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳}} | Construction year = [[687]] (first year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}) | Dimensions = East-west 58m
Height 9m | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi" | Excavated items = | image = [[File:Noguchi Obo Kofun, haisho.jpg|300px]]
Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} at Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}, dating to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and the 41st Emperor {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in shape, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound shape is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with {{ill|Ashlar|ja|切石積み}}, reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor {{ill|Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Tumulus|ja|御廟野古墳}}). However, the tomb was plundered in {{ill|1235|ja|文暦2年}}, during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient Period === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of {{ill|687|ja|持統元年}}, shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November {{ill|688|ja|持統2年}}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|ja|大宝2年}} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|ja|大宝3年}}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern Era === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and this designation remains to this day. == Construction and Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin Burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}} of {{ill|646|ja|大化2年}} restricted the construction of large burial mounds, yet high-ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/040/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Emperor Tenmu] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/041/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Empress Jitō] - Imperial Household Agency [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Octagonal tumuli]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Empress Jitō]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Burial Mound]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Noguchi-ōsaka Burial Mound-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Burial Mound]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Burial Mound]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Noguchi-ōsaka Burial Mound-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Burial Mound]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Burial Mound]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:野口王墓]]

Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} at Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}, dating to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and the 41st Emperor {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in shape, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound shape is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with {{ill|Ashlar|ja|切石積み}}, reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor {{ill|Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Tumulus|ja|御廟野古墳}}). However, the tomb was plundered in {{ill|1235|ja|文暦2年}}, during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient Period === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of {{ill|687|ja|持統元年}}, shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November {{ill|688|ja|持統2年}}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|ja|大宝2年}} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|ja|大宝3年}}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern Era === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and this designation remains to this day. == Construction and Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin Burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}} of {{ill|646|ja|大化2年}} restricted the construction of large burial mounds, yet high-ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == Mound-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Burial Mound]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[ja:野口王墓]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Noguchi Royal Tomb | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|7.52|N|135|48|28.17|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Noguchi Royal Tomb (designated as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum)}} | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳}} | Construction year = [[687]] (first year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}) | Dimensions = East-west 58m
Height 9m | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi" | Excavated items = | image = [[File:Noguchi Obo Kofun, haisho.jpg|300px]]
Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} at Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}, dating to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and the 41st Emperor {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in shape, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound shape is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with {{ill|Ashlar|ja|切石積み}}, reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor {{ill|Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Tumulus|ja|御廟野古墳}}). However, the tomb was plundered in {{ill|1235|ja|文暦2年}}, during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient Period === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of {{ill|687|ja|持統元年}}, shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November {{ill|688|ja|持統2年}}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|ja|大宝2年}} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|ja|大宝3年}}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern Era === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and this designation remains to this day. == Construction and Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin Burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}} of {{ill|646|ja|大化2年}} restricted the construction of large burial mounds, yet high-ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} at Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}, dating to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and the 41st Emperor {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in shape, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound shape is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with {{ill|Ashlar|ja|切石積み}}, reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor {{ill|Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Tumulus|ja|御廟野古墳}}). However, the tomb was plundered in {{ill|1235|ja|文暦2年}}, during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient Period === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of {{ill|687|ja|持統元年}}, shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November {{ill|688|ja|持統2年}}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|ja|大宝2年}} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|ja|大宝3年}}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern Era === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and this designation remains to this day. == Construction and Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin Burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}} of {{ill|646|ja|大化2年}} restricted the construction of large burial mounds, yet high-ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == Mound-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Burial Mound]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[ja:野口王墓]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Noguchi Royal Tomb | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|7.52|N|135|48|28.17|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Noguchi Royal Tomb (designated as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum)}} | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳}} | Construction year = [[687]] (first year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}) | Dimensions = East-west 58m
Height 9m | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi" | Excavated items = | image = [[File:Noguchi Obo Kofun, haisho.jpg|300px]]
Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} at Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}, dating to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and the 41st Emperor {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in shape, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound shape is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with {{ill|Ashlar|ja|切石積み}}, reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor {{ill|Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Tumulus|ja|御廟野古墳}}). However, the tomb was plundered in {{ill|1235|ja|文暦2年}}, during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient Period === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of {{ill|687|ja|持統元年}}, shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November {{ill|688|ja|持統2年}}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|ja|大宝2年}} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|ja|大宝3年}}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern Era === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and this designation remains to this day. == Construction and Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin Burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}} of {{ill|646|ja|大化2年}} restricted the construction of large burial mounds, yet high-ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == Mound-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Burial Mound]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[ja:野口王墓]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Noguchi Royal Tomb | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|7.52|N|135|48|28.17|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Noguchi Royal Tomb (designated as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum)}} | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳}} | Construction year = [[687]] (first year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}) | Dimensions = East-west 58m
Height 9m | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi" | Excavated items = | image = [[File:Noguchi Obo Kofun, haisho.jpg|300px]]
Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} at Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}, dating to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and the 41st Emperor {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in shape, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound shape is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with {{ill|Ashlar|ja|切石積み}}, reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor {{ill|Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Tumulus|ja|御廟野古墳}}). However, the tomb was plundered in {{ill|1235|ja|文暦2年}}, during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient Period === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of {{ill|687|ja|持統元年}}, shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November {{ill|688|ja|持統2年}}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|ja|大宝2年}} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|ja|大宝3年}}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern Era === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and this designation remains to this day. == Construction and Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin Burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}} of {{ill|646|ja|大化2年}} restricted the construction of large burial mounds, yet high-ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern Era === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and this designation remains to this day. == Construction and Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin Burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}} of {{ill|646|ja|大化2年}} restricted the construction of large burial mounds, yet high-ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Noguchi Royal Tomb | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|7.52|N|135|48|28.17|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Noguchi Royal Tomb (designated as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum)}} | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳}} | Construction year = [[687]] (first year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}) | Dimensions = East-west 58m
Height 9m | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi" | Excavated items = | image = [[File:Noguchi Obo Kofun, haisho.jpg|300px]]
Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} at Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}, dating to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and the 41st Emperor {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in shape, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound shape is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with {{ill|Ashlar|ja|切石積み}}, reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor {{ill|Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Tumulus|ja|御廟野古墳}}). However, the tomb was plundered in {{ill|1235|ja|文暦2年}}, during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient Period === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of {{ill|687|ja|持統元年}}, shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November {{ill|688|ja|持統2年}}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|ja|大宝2年}} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|ja|大宝3年}}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern Era === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and this designation remains to this day. == Construction and Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin Burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}} of {{ill|646|ja|大化2年}} restricted the construction of large burial mounds, yet high-ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == Mound-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Burial Mound]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[ja:野口王墓]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Noguchi Royal Tomb | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|7.52|N|135|48|28.17|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Noguchi Royal Tomb (designated as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum)}} | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳}} | Construction year = [[687]] (first year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}) | Dimensions = East-west 58m
Height 9m | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi" | Excavated items = | image = [[File:Noguchi Obo Kofun, haisho.jpg|300px]]
Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} at Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}, dating to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and the 41st Emperor {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in shape, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound shape is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with {{ill|Ashlar|ja|切石積み}}, reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor {{ill|Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Tumulus|ja|御廟野古墳}}). However, the tomb was plundered in {{ill|1235|ja|文暦2年}}, during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient Period === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of {{ill|687|ja|持統元年}}, shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November {{ill|688|ja|持統2年}}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|ja|大宝2年}} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|ja|大宝3年}}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern Era === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and this designation remains to this day. == Construction and Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin Burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}} of {{ill|646|ja|大化2年}} restricted the construction of large burial mounds, yet high-ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/040/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Emperor Tenmu] - Imperial Household Agency * {{Infobox ancient site | name = Noguchi Royal Tomb | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|7.52|N|135|48|28.17|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Noguchi Royal Tomb (designated as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum)}} | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳}} | Construction year = [[687]] (first year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}) | Dimensions = East-west 58m
Height 9m | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi" | Excavated items = | image = [[File:Noguchi Obo Kofun, haisho.jpg|300px]]
Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} at Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}, dating to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and the 41st Emperor {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in shape, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound shape is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with {{ill|Ashlar|ja|切石積み}}, reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor {{ill|Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Tumulus|ja|御廟野古墳}}). However, the tomb was plundered in {{ill|1235|ja|文暦2年}}, during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient Period === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of {{ill|687|ja|持統元年}}, shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November {{ill|688|ja|持統2年}}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|ja|大宝2年}} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|ja|大宝3年}}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern Era === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and this designation remains to this day. == Construction and Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin Burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}} of {{ill|646|ja|大化2年}} restricted the construction of large burial mounds, yet high-ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}, dating to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and the 41st Emperor {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in shape, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound shape is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with {{ill|Ashlar|ja|切石積み}}, reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor {{ill|Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Tumulus|ja|御廟野古墳}}). However, the tomb was plundered in {{ill|1235|ja|文暦2年}}, during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient Period === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of {{ill|687|ja|持統元年}}, shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November {{ill|688|ja|持統2年}}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|ja|大宝2年}} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|ja|大宝3年}}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern Era === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and this designation remains to this day. == Construction and Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin Burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}} of {{ill|646|ja|大化2年}} restricted the construction of large burial mounds, yet high-ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == He was interred in November {{ill|688|ja|持統2年}}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|ja|大宝2年}} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|ja|大宝3年}}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern Era === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and this designation remains to this day. == Construction and Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin Burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}} of {{ill|646|ja|大化2年}} restricted the construction of large burial mounds, yet high-ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == Mound-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Burial Mound]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[ja:野口王墓]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Noguchi Royal Tomb | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|7.52|N|135|48|28.17|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Noguchi Royal Tomb (designated as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum)}} | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | Construction year = [[687]] (first year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}) | Dimensions = East-west 58m
Height 9m | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi" | Excavated items = | image = [[File:Noguchi Obo Kofun, haisho.jpg|300px]]
Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} at Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}, dating to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and the 41st Emperor {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in shape, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound shape is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with {{ill|Ashlar|ja|切石積み}}, reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor {{ill|Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Tumulus|ja|御廟野古墳|1=Gobyō-no-Kofun}}). However, the tomb was plundered in {{ill|1235|ja|文暦2年}}, during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient Period === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of {{ill|687|ja|持統元年}}, shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November {{ill|688|ja|持統2年}}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|ja|大宝2年}} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|ja|大宝3年}}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern Era === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and this designation remains to this day. == Construction and Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin Burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}} of {{ill|646|ja|大化2年}} restricted the construction of large burial mounds, yet high-ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/040/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Emperor Tenmu] - Imperial Household Agency * Mound-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Burial Mound]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[ja:野口王墓]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Noguchi Royal Tomb | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|7.52|N|135|48|28.17|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Noguchi Royal Tomb (designated as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum)}} | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | Construction year = [[687]] (first year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}) | Dimensions = East-west 58m
Height 9m | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi" | Excavated items = | image = [[File:Noguchi Obo Kofun, haisho.jpg|300px]]
Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} at Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}, dating to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and the 41st Emperor {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in shape, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound shape is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with {{ill|Ashlar|ja|切石積み}}, reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor {{ill|Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Tumulus|ja|御廟野古墳|1=Gobyō-no-Kofun}}). However, the tomb was plundered in {{ill|1235|ja|文暦2年}}, during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient Period === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of {{ill|687|ja|持統元年}}, shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November {{ill|688|ja|持統2年}}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|ja|大宝2年}} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|ja|大宝3年}}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern Era === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and this designation remains to this day. == Construction and Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin Burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}} of {{ill|646|ja|大化2年}} restricted the construction of large burial mounds, yet high-ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == Mound-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Burial Mound]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[ja:野口王墓]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Noguchi Royal Tomb | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|7.52|N|135|48|28.17|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Noguchi Royal Tomb (designated as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum)}} | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | Construction year = [[687]] (first year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}) | Dimensions = East-west 58m
Height 9m | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi" | Excavated items = | image = [[File:Noguchi Obo Kofun, haisho.jpg|300px]]
Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} at Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}, dating to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and the 41st Emperor {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in shape, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound shape is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with {{ill|Ashlar|ja|切石積み}}, reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor {{ill|Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Tumulus|ja|御廟野古墳|1=Gobyō-no-Kofun}}). However, the tomb was plundered in {{ill|1235|ja|文暦2年}}, during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient Period === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of {{ill|687|ja|持統元年}}, shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November {{ill|688|ja|持統2年}}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|ja|大宝2年}} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|ja|大宝3年}}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern Era === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and this designation remains to this day. == Construction and Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin Burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}} of {{ill|646|ja|大化2年}} restricted the construction of large burial mounds, yet high-ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == deprecated close]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Burial Mound]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[ja:野口王墓]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Noguchi Royal Tomb | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|7.52|N|135|48|28.17|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Noguchi Royal Tomb (designated as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum)}} | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | Construction year = [[687]] (first year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}) | Dimensions = East-west 58m
Height 9m | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi" | Excavated items = | image = [[File:Noguchi Obo Kofun, haisho.jpg|300px]]
Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} at Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}, dating to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and the 41st Emperor {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in shape, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound shape is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with {{ill|Ashlar|ja|切石積み}}, reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor {{ill|Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Tumulus|ja|御廟野古墳|1=Gobyō-no-Kofun}}). However, the tomb was plundered in {{ill|1235|ja|文暦2年}}, during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient Period === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of {{ill|687|ja|持統元年}}, shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November {{ill|688|ja|持統2年}}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|ja|大宝2年}} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|ja|大宝3年}}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern Era === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and this designation remains to this day. == Construction and Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin Burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}} of {{ill|646|ja|大化2年}} restricted the construction of large burial mounds, yet high-ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Noguchi Royal Tomb | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|7.52|N|135|48|28.17|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Noguchi Royal Tomb (designated as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum)}} | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | Construction year = [[687]] (first year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}) | Dimensions = East-west 58m
Height 9m | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi" | Excavated items = | image = [[File:Noguchi Obo Kofun, haisho.jpg|300px]]
Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} at Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}, dating to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and the 41st Emperor {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in shape, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound shape is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with {{ill|Ashlar|ja|切石積み}}, reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor {{ill|Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Tumulus|ja|御廟野古墳|1=Gobyō-no-Kofun}}). However, the tomb was plundered in {{ill|1235|ja|文暦2年}}, during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient Period === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of {{ill|687|ja|持統元年}}, shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November {{ill|688|ja|持統2年}}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|ja|大宝2年}} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|ja|大宝3年}}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern Era === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14年}}, and this designation remains to this day. == Construction and Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin Burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}} of {{ill|646|ja|大化2年}} restricted the construction of large burial mounds, yet high-ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} ==
