Height 9m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} other_designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} other_designation "Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi" | excavations = | image = [[File:Noguchi Obo Kofun, haisho.jpg|300px]]
Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} at Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=28|coordinates=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|map_dot_label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}, dating to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has other_designation1 it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and the 41st Emperor {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in type, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound type is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with {{ill|Ashlar|ja|切石積み}}, reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor {{ill|Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Kofun|ja|御廟野古墳|1=Gobyō-no-Kofun}}). However, the tomb was plundered in {{ill|1235|ja|文暦2nd year }}, during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient built === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of {{ill|687|ja|持統元年}}, shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November {{ill|688|ja|持統2nd year }}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|ja|大宝2nd year }} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|ja|大宝3rd year }}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern built === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14th year }}, and this other_designation remains to this day. == Construction and area == The mound's area are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}} of {{ill|646|ja|大化2nd year }} restricted the construction of large Kofuns, yet high-ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == Height 9m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} other_designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} other_designation "Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi" | excavations = | image = [[File:Noguchi Obo Kofun, haisho.jpg|300px]]
Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} at Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=28|coordinates=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|map_dot_label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}, dating to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has other_designation1 it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and the 41st Emperor {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in type, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound type is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with {{ill|Ashlar|ja|切石積み}}, reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor {{ill|Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Kofun|ja|御廟野古墳|1=Gobyō-no-Kofun}}). However, the tomb was plundered in {{ill|1235|ja|文暦2nd year }}, during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient built === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of {{ill|687|ja|持統元年}}, shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November {{ill|688|ja|持統2nd year }}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|ja|大宝2nd year }} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|ja|大宝3rd year }}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern built === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14th year }}, and this other_designation remains to this day. == Construction and area == The mound's area are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}} of {{ill|646|ja|大化2nd year }} restricted the construction of large Kofuns, yet high-ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/040/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Emperor Tenmu] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/041/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Empress Jitō] - Imperial Household Agency [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Asuka, Nara]] Height 9m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Historic Site "Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi" | excavations = | image = [[File:Noguchi Obo Kofun, haisho.jpg|300px]]
Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} at Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=28|coordinates=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|map_dot_label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}, dating to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and the 41st Emperor {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in type, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound type is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with {{ill|Ashlar|ja|切石積み}}, reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor {{ill|Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Kofun|ja|御廟野古墳|1=Gobyō-no-Kofun}}). However, the tomb was plundered in {{ill|1235|ja|文暦2nd year }}, during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient built === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of {{ill|687|ja|持統元年}}, shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November {{ill|688|ja|持統2nd year }}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|ja|大宝2nd year }} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|ja|大宝3rd year }}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern built === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14th year }}, and this Historic Site remains to this day. == Construction and area == The mound's area are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}} of {{ill|646|ja|大化2nd year }} restricted the construction of large Kofuns, yet high-ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]][[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]][[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]][[ja:野口王墓]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Noguchi Royal Tomb | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|7.52|N|135|48|28.17|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Noguchi Royal Tomb (Historic Site1 as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum)}} | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | Construction year = [[687]] (first year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}) | area = East-west 58m
Height 9m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Historic Site "Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi" | excavations = | image = [[File:Noguchi Obo Kofun, haisho.jpg|300px]]
Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} at Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=28|coordinates=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|map_dot_label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}, dating to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and the 41st Emperor {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in type, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound type is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with {{ill|Ashlar|ja|切石積み}}, reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor {{ill|Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Kofun|ja|御廟野古墳|1=Gobyō-no-Kofun}}). However, the tomb was plundered in {{ill|1235|ja|文暦2nd year }}, during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient built === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of {{ill|687|ja|持統元年}}, shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November {{ill|688|ja|持統2nd year }}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|ja|大宝2nd year }} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|ja|大宝3rd year }}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern built === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14th year }}, and this Historic Site remains to this day. == Construction and area == The mound's area are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}} of {{ill|646|ja|大化2nd year }} restricted the construction of large Kofuns, yet high-ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/040/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Emperor Tenmu] - Imperial Household Agency * This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Noguchi Royal Tomb | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|7.52|N|135|48|28.17|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Noguchi Royal Tomb (Historic Site1 as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum)}} | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | Construction year = [[687]] (first year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}) | area = East-west 58m
Height 9m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Historic Site "Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi" | excavations = | image = [[File:Noguchi Obo Kofun, haisho.jpg|300px]]
Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} at Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=28|coordinates=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|map_dot_label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}, dating to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and the 41st Emperor {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in type, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound type is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with {{ill|Ashlar|ja|切石積み}}, reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor {{ill|Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Kofun|ja|御廟野古墳|1=Gobyō-no-Kofun}}). However, the tomb was plundered in {{ill|1235|ja|文暦2nd year }}, during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient built === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of {{ill|687|ja|持統元年}}, shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November {{ill|688|ja|持統2nd year }}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|ja|大宝2nd year }} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|ja|大宝3rd year }}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern built === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14th year }}, and this Historic Site remains to this day. == Construction and area == The mound's area are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}} of {{ill|646|ja|大化2nd year }} restricted the construction of large Kofuns, yet high-ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/040/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Emperor Tenmu] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/041/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Empress Jitō] - Imperial Household Agency {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Octagonal tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Empress Jitō]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]] [[ja:野口王墓]] [[sn:oguchi-ōsaka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun}}

Height 9m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} Historic Site "Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi" | excavations = | image = [[File:Noguchi Obo Kofun, haisho.jpg|300px]]
Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}} at Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=28|coordinates=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|map_dot_label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}}, dating to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}} and the 41st Emperor {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in type, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound type is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with {{ill|Ashlar|ja|切石積み|12=draft}}, reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|12=draft}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor {{ill|Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft}}'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Kofun|ja|御廟野古墳|1=Gobyō-no-Kofun|12=draft}}). However, the tomb was plundered in {{ill|1235|ja|文暦2nd year|12=draft}}, during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient built === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of {{ill|687|ja|持統元年|12=draft}}, shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November {{ill|688|ja|持統2nd year|12=draft}}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|ja|大宝2nd year|12=draft}} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|ja|大宝3rd year|12=draft}}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家|12=draft}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|12=draft}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記|12=draft}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern built === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵|12=draft}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14th year|12=draft}}, and this Historic Site remains to this day. == Construction and area == The mound's area are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|12=draft}} of {{ill|646|ja|大化2nd year|12=draft}} restricted the construction of large Kofuns, yet high-ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/040/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Emperor Tenmu] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/041/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Empress Jitō] - Imperial Household Agency [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Octagonal tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Empress Jitō]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]] [[ja:野口王墓]] [[sn:oguchi-ōsaka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Noguchi Royal Tomb | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}{{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft}} | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|7.52|N|135|48|28.17|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Noguchi Royal Tomb (Historic Site1 as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum)}} | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} | Construction year = [[687]] (first year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}}) | area = East-west 58m
Height 9m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} Historic Site "Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi" | excavations = | image = [[File:Noguchi Obo Kofun, haisho.jpg|300px]]
Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}} at Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=28|coordinates=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|map_dot_label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}}, dating to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}} and the 41st Emperor {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in type, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound type is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with {{ill|Ashlar|ja|切石積み|12=draft}}, reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|12=draft}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor {{ill|Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft}}'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Kofun|ja|御廟野古墳|1=Gobyō-no-Kofun|12=draft}}). However, the tomb was plundered in {{ill|1235|ja|文暦2nd year|12=draft}}, during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient built === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of {{ill|687|ja|持統元年|12=draft}}, shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November {{ill|688|ja|持統2nd year|12=draft}}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|ja|大宝2nd year|12=draft}} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|ja|大宝3rd year|12=draft}}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家|12=draft}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|12=draft}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記|12=draft}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern built === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵|12=draft}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14th year|12=draft}}, and this Historic Site remains to this day. == Construction and area == The mound's area are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|12=draft}} of {{ill|646|ja|大化2nd year|12=draft}} restricted the construction of large Kofuns, yet high-ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/040/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Emperor Tenmu] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/041/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Empress Jitō] - Imperial Household Agency [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Octagonal tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Empress Jitō]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]] [[ja:野口王墓]] [[sn:oguchi-ōsaka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Noguchi Royal Tomb | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}{{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft}} | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|7.52|N|135|48|28.17|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Noguchi Royal Tomb (Historic Site1 as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum)}} | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} | Construction year = [[687]] (first year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}}) | area = East-west 58m
Height 9m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} Historic Site "Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi" | excavations = | image = [[File:Noguchi Obo Kofun, haisho.jpg|300px]]
Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}} at Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=28|coordinates=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|map_dot_label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}}, dating to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}} and the 41st Emperor {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in type, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound type is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with {{ill|Ashlar|ja|切石積み|12=draft}}, reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|12=draft}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor {{ill|Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft}}'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Kofun|ja|御廟野古墳|1=Gobyō-no-Kofun|12=draft}}). However, the tomb was plundered in {{ill|1235|ja|文暦2nd year|12=draft}}, during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient built === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of {{ill|687|ja|持統元年|12=draft}}, shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November {{ill|688|ja|持統2nd year|12=draft}}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|ja|大宝2nd year|12=draft}} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|ja|大宝3rd year|12=draft}}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家|12=draft}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|12=draft}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記|12=draft}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern built === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵|12=draft}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14th year|12=draft}}, and this Historic Site remains to this day. == Construction and area == The mound's area are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|12=draft}} of {{ill|646|ja|大化2nd year|12=draft}} restricted the construction of large Kofuns, yet high-ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/040/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Emperor Tenmu] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/041/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Empress Jitō] - Imperial Household Agency [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Octagonal tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Empress Jitō]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]] [[ja:野口王墓]] [[sn:oguchi-ōsaka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}}

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Noguchi Royal Tomb | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}{{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft}} | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|7.52|N|135|48|28.17|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Noguchi Royal Tomb (Historic Site1 as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum)}} | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} | Construction year = [[687]] (first year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}}) | area = East-west 58m
Height 9m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} Historic Site "Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi" | excavations = | image = [[File:Noguchi Obo Kofun, haisho.jpg|300px]]
Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}} at Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=28|coordinates=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|map_dot_label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}}, dating to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}} and the 41st Emperor {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in type, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound type is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with {{ill|Ashlar|ja|切石積み|12=draft}}, reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|12=draft}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor {{ill|Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft}}'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Kofun|ja|御廟野古墳|1=Gobyō-no-Kofun|12=draft}}). However, the tomb was plundered in {{ill|1235|ja|文暦2nd year|12=draft}}, during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient built === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of {{ill|687|ja|持統元年|12=draft}}, shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November {{ill|688|ja|持統2nd year|12=draft}}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|ja|大宝2nd year|12=draft}} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|ja|大宝3rd year|12=draft}}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家|12=draft}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|12=draft}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記|12=draft}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern built === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵|12=draft}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14th year|12=draft}}, and this Historic Site remains to this day. == Construction and area == The mound's area are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|12=draft}} of {{ill|646|ja|大化2nd year|12=draft}} restricted the construction of large Kofuns, yet high-ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/040/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Emperor Tenmu] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/041/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Empress Jitō] - Imperial Household Agency [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Octagonal tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Empress Jitō]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]] [[ja:野口王墓]] [[sn:oguchi-ōsaka Kofun]] {{citation needed|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Noguchi Royal Tomb | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}{{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft}} | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|7.52|N|135|48|28.17|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Noguchi Royal Tomb (Historic Site1 as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum)}} | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} | Construction year = [[687]] (first year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}}) | area = East-west 58m
Height 9m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} Historic Site "Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi" | excavations = | image = [[File:Noguchi Obo Kofun, haisho.jpg|300px]]
Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}} at Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=28|coordinates=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|map_dot_label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}}, dating to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}} and the 41st Emperor {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in type, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound type is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with {{ill|Ashlar|ja|切石積み|12=draft}}, reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|12=draft}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor {{ill|Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft}}'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Kofun|ja|御廟野古墳|1=Gobyō-no-Kofun|12=draft}}). However, the tomb was plundered in {{ill|1235|ja|文暦2nd year|12=draft}}, during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient built === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of {{ill|687|ja|持統元年|12=draft}}, shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November {{ill|688|ja|持統2nd year|12=draft}}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|ja|大宝2nd year|12=draft}} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|ja|大宝3rd year|12=draft}}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家|12=draft}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|12=draft}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記|12=draft}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern built === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵|12=draft}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14th year|12=draft}}, and this Historic Site remains to this day. == Construction and area == The mound's area are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|12=draft}} of {{ill|646|ja|大化2nd year|12=draft}} restricted the construction of large Kofuns, yet high-ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/040/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Emperor Tenmu] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/041/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Empress Jitō] - Imperial Household Agency [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Octagonal tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Empress Jitō]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]] [[ja:野口王墓]] [[sn:oguchi-ōsaka Kofun]] {{citation needed|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Noguchi Royal Tomb | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft|12=draft}} | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|7.52|N|135|48|28.17|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Noguchi Royal Tomb (Historic Site1 as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum)}} | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | built year = [[687]] (first year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}) | area = East-west 58m
Height 9m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} Historic Site "Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi" | excavations = | image = [[File:Noguchi Obo Kofun, haisho.jpg|300px]]
Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} at Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=28|coordinates=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|map_dot_label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, dating to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft|12=draft}} and the 41st Emperor {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}} refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in type, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound type is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with {{ill|Ashlar|ja|切石積み|12=draft|12=draft}}, reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|12=draft|12=draft}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor {{ill|Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Kofun|ja|御廟野古墳|1=Gobyō-no-Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}). However, the tomb was plundered in {{ill|1235|ja|文暦2nd year|12=draft|12=draft}}, during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient built === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}} records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of {{ill|687|ja|持統元年|12=draft|12=draft}}, shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November {{ill|688|ja|持統2nd year|12=draft|12=draft}}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|ja|大宝2nd year|12=draft|12=draft}} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|ja|大宝3rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家|12=draft|12=draft}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|12=draft|12=draft}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記|12=draft|12=draft}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern built === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵|12=draft|12=draft}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, and this Historic Site remains to this day. == built and area == The mound's area are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|646|ja|大化2nd year|12=draft|12=draft}} restricted the construction of large Kofuns, yet high-ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/040/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Emperor Tenmu] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/041/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Empress Jitō] - Imperial Household Agency {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Octagonal tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Empress Jitō]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]] [[ja:野口王墓]] [[sn:oguchi-ōsaka Kofun]]

The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/040/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Emperor Tenmu] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/041/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Empress Jitō] - Imperial Household Agency {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Octagonal tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Empress Jitō]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]] [[ja:野口王墓]] [[sn:oguchi-ōsaka Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Noguchi Royal Tomb | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft|12=draft}} | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|7.52|N|135|48|28.17|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Noguchi Royal Tomb (Historic Site1 as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum)}} | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | built year = [[687]] (first year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}) | area = East-west 58m
Height 9m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} Historic Site "Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi" | excavations = | image = [[File:Noguchi Obo Kofun, haisho.jpg|300px]]
Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} at Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=28|coordinates=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|map_dot_label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, dating to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft|12=draft}} and the 41st Emperor {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}} refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in type, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound type is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with {{ill|Ashlar|ja|切石積み|12=draft|12=draft}}, reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|12=draft|12=draft}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor {{ill|Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Kofun|ja|御廟野古墳|1=Gobyō-no-Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}). However, the tomb was plundered in {{ill|1235|ja|文暦2nd year|12=draft|12=draft}}, during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient built === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}} records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of {{ill|687|ja|持統元年|12=draft|12=draft}}, shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November {{ill|688|ja|持統2nd year|12=draft|12=draft}}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|ja|大宝2nd year|12=draft|12=draft}} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|ja|大宝3rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家|12=draft|12=draft}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|12=draft|12=draft}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記|12=draft|12=draft}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern built === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵|12=draft|12=draft}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|ja|明治14th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, and this Historic Site remains to this day. == built and area == The mound's area are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|646|ja|大化2nd year|12=draft|12=draft}} restricted the construction of large Kofuns, yet high-ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/040/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Emperor Tenmu] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/041/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Empress Jitō] - Imperial Household Agency {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Octagonal tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Empress Jitō]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun]] [[ja:野口王墓]] [[sn:oguchi-ōsaka Kofun]]
