It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dating to the terminal phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and the 41st Emperor [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]]. The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in type, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound type is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with [[:en:Ashlar|Ashlar]], reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquered coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Tenji]]'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Kofun|ja|御廟野古墳|1=Gobyō-no-Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyō-no Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyō-no-Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). However, the tomb was plundered in [[:en:1235|1235]], during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient built === The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of [[:en:687|687]], shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November [[:en:688|688]]. Empress Jitō passed away in [[:en:702|702]] and was cremated at Asukaoka in December [[:en:703|703]], after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in [[:en:Fujiwara no Teika|Fujiwara no Teika]]'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern built === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Restoration|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in [[:en:1881|Meiji 14]], and this Historic Site remains to this day. == built and area == The mound's area are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thin burial Decree|1=Thin Burial Edict|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622174}} of [[:en:646|646]] restricted the construction of large Kofuns, yet high-Ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] and [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]], demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/040/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Emperor Tenmu] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/041/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Empress Jitō] - Imperial Household Agency {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|野口王墓|version=103308017|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Empress Jitō]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:野口王墓]] [[Category:Octagonal Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Empress Jitō]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] {{Commons category|Noguchi Obo Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11645194}}

This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Noguchi Royal Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.468756|N|135.807825|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | built year = [[:en:687|687]] (first year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]) | area = East-west 58m
Height 9m | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] | Imperial tomb = [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site "Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi" | excavations = | image = File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=28|coordinates=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|map_dot_label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|octagonal kofun|WD=Q11391751}} | commemorates = {{ill|Jitō|WD=Q232026}}; {{ill|Tenmu|WD=Q335181}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dating to the terminal phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and the 41st Emperor [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]]. The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in type, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound type is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with [[:en:Ashlar|Ashlar]], reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquered coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Tenji]]'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Kofun|ja|御廟野古墳|1=Gobyō-no-Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyō-no Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyō-no-Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). However, the tomb was plundered in [[:en:1235|1235]], during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient built === The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of [[:en:687|687]], shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November [[:en:688|688]]. Empress Jitō passed away in [[:en:702|702]] and was cremated at Asukaoka in December [[:en:703|703]], after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in [[:en:Fujiwara no Teika|Fujiwara no Teika]]'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern built === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Restoration|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in [[:en:1881|Meiji 14]], and this Historic Site remains to this day. == built and area == The mound's area are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thin burial Decree|1=Thin Burial Edict|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622174}} of [[:en:646|646]] restricted the construction of large Kofuns, yet high-Ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] and [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]], demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/040/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Emperor Tenmu] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/041/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Empress Jitō] - Imperial Household Agency {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|野口王墓|version=103308017|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Empress Jitō]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:野口王墓]] [[Category:Octagonal Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Empress Jitō]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] {{Commons category|Noguchi Obo Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11645194}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] and [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]], demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/040/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Emperor Tenmu] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/041/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Empress Jitō] - Imperial Household Agency {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|野口王墓|version=103308017|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Empress Jitō]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:野口王墓]] [[Category:Octagonal Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Empress Jitō]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] {{Commons category|Noguchi Obo Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11645194}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Noguchi Obo Kofun]][[ja:野口王墓]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11645194]] Wikidata name: Noguchi Royal Tomb [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Noguchi Royal Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.468756|N|135.807825|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | built year = [[:en:687|687]] (first year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]) | area = East-west 58m
Height 9m | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] | Imperial tomb = [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site "Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi" | excavations = | image = File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=28|coordinates=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|map_dot_label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|octagonal kofun|WD=Q11391751}} | commemorates = {{ill|Jitō|WD=Q232026}}; {{ill|Tenmu|WD=Q335181}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dating to the terminal phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and the 41st Emperor [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]]. The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in type, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound type is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with [[:en:Ashlar|Ashlar]], reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquered coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Tenji]]'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Kofun|ja|御廟野古墳|1=Gobyō-no-Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyō-no Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyō-no-Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). However, the tomb was plundered in [[:en:1235|1235]], during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient built === The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of [[:en:687|687]], shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November [[:en:688|688]]. Empress Jitō passed away in [[:en:702|702]] and was cremated at Asukaoka in December [[:en:703|703]], after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in [[:en:Fujiwara no Teika|Fujiwara no Teika]]'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern built === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Restoration|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in [[:en:1881|Meiji 14]], and this Historic Site remains to this day. == built and area == The mound's area are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thin burial Decree|1=Thin Burial Edict|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622174}} of [[:en:646|646]] restricted the construction of large Kofuns, yet high-Ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] and [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]], demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/040/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Emperor Tenmu] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/041/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Empress Jitō] - Imperial Household Agency {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|野口王墓|version=103308017|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Empress Jitō]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:野口王墓]] [[Category:Octagonal Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Empress Jitō]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] {{Commons category|Noguchi Obo Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11645194}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dating to the terminal phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and the 41st Emperor [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]]. The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in type, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound type is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with [[:en:Ashlar|Ashlar]], reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquered coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Tenji]]'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Kofun|ja|御廟野古墳|1=Gobyō-no-Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyō-no Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyō-no-Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). However, the tomb was plundered in [[:en:1235|1235]], during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient built === The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of [[:en:687|687]], shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November [[:en:688|688]]. Empress Jitō passed away in [[:en:702|702]] and was cremated at Asukaoka in December [[:en:703|703]], after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in [[:en:Fujiwara no Teika|Fujiwara no Teika]]'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern built === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Restoration|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in [[:en:1881|Meiji 14]], and this Historic Site remains to this day. == built and area == The mound's area are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thin burial Decree|1=Thin Burial Edict|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622174}} of [[:en:646|646]] restricted the construction of large Kofuns, yet high-Ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] and [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]], demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/040/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Emperor Tenmu] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/041/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Empress Jitō] - Imperial Household Agency {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|野口王墓|version=103308017|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Empress Jitō]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:野口王墓]] [[Category:Octagonal Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Empress Jitō]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] {{Commons category|Noguchi Obo Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11645194}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Noguchi Royal Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.468756|N|135.807825|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | built year = [[:en:687|687]] (first year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]) | area = East-west 58m
Height 9m | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] | Imperial tomb = [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site "Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi" | excavations = | image = File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=28|coordinates=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|map_dot_label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|octagonal kofun|WD=Q11391751}} | commemorates = {{ill|Jitō|WD=Q232026}}; {{ill|Tenmu|WD=Q335181}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dating to the terminal phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and the 41st Emperor [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]]. The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in type, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound type is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with [[:en:Ashlar|Ashlar]], reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquered coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Tenji]]'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Kofun|ja|御廟野古墳|1=Gobyō-no-Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyō-no Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyō-no-Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). However, the tomb was plundered in [[:en:1235|1235]], during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient built === The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of [[:en:687|687]], shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November [[:en:688|688]]. Empress Jitō passed away in [[:en:702|702]] and was cremated at Asukaoka in December [[:en:703|703]], after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in [[:en:Fujiwara no Teika|Fujiwara no Teika]]'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern built === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Restoration|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in [[:en:1881|Meiji 14]], and this Historic Site remains to this day. == built and area == The mound's area are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thin burial Decree|1=Thin Burial Edict|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622174}} of [[:en:646|646]] restricted the construction of large Kofuns, yet high-Ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] and [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]], demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/040/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Emperor Tenmu] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/041/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Empress Jitō] - Imperial Household Agency {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|野口王墓|version=103308017|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:野口王墓]] {{Commons category|Noguchi Obo Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11645194}} [[Category:Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Empress Jitō]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Octagonal Kofun]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] and [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]], demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/040/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Emperor Tenmu] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/041/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Empress Jitō] - Imperial Household Agency {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|野口王墓|version=103308017|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:野口王墓]] {{Commons category|Noguchi Obo Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11645194}} [[Category:Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Empress Jitō]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Octagonal Kofun]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Noguchi Obo Kofun]][[ja:野口王墓]] [[co:commons:Category:Noguchi Obo Kofun]][[ja:野口王墓]] [[sn:commons:Category:Noguchi Obo Kofun]][[ja:野口王墓]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11645194]] Wikidata name: Noguchi Royal Tomb [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Noguchi Royal Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.468756|N|135.807825|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | built year = [[:en:687|687]] (first year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]) | area = East-west 58m
Height 9m | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] | Imperial tomb = [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site "Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi" | excavations = | image = File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=28|coordinates=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|map_dot_label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|octagonal kofun|WD=Q11391751}} | commemorates = {{ill|Jitō|WD=Q232026}}; {{ill|Tenmu|WD=Q335181}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dating to the terminal phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and the 41st Emperor [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]]. The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in type, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound type is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with [[:en:Ashlar|Ashlar]], reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquered coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Tenji]]'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Kofun|ja|御廟野古墳|1=Gobyō-no-Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyō-no Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyō-no-Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). However, the tomb was plundered in [[:en:1235|1235]], during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient built === The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of [[:en:687|687]], shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November [[:en:688|688]]. Empress Jitō passed away in [[:en:702|702]] and was cremated at Asukaoka in December [[:en:703|703]], after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in [[:en:Fujiwara no Teika|Fujiwara no Teika]]'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern built === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Restoration|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in [[:en:1881|Meiji 14]], and this Historic Site remains to this day. == built and area == The mound's area are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thin burial Decree|1=Thin Burial Edict|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622174}} of [[:en:646|646]] restricted the construction of large Kofuns, yet high-Ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] and [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]], demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/040/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Emperor Tenmu] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/041/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Empress Jitō] - Imperial Household Agency {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|野口王墓|version=103308017|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:野口王墓]] {{Commons category|Noguchi Obo Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11645194}} [[Category:Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Empress Jitō]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Octagonal Kofun]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Height 9m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} Historic Site "Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi" | excavations = | image = File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=28|coordinates=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|map_dot_label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|octagonal kofun|WD=Q11391751}} | commemorates = {{ill|Jitō|WD=Q232026}}; {{ill|Tenmu|WD=Q335181}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dating to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and the 41st Emperor {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Jitō}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in type, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound type is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with {{ill|Ashlar|en|Ashlar|WD=Q1122603|lt=Ashlar}}, reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquered coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Tenji}}'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Kofun|ja|御廟野古墳|1=Gobyō-no-Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyō-no Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyō-no-Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). However, the tomb was plundered in {{ill|1235|en|1235|WD=Q5425|lt=1235}}, during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient built === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of {{ill|687|en|687|WD=Q30303|lt=687}}, shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November {{ill|688|en|688|WD=Q30443|lt=688}}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|en|702|WD=Q23497|lt=702}} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|en|703|WD=Q23500|lt=703}}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|en|Fujiwara no Teika|WD=Q378425|lt=Fujiwara no Teika}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern built === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Restoration|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|en|1881|WD=Q7808|lt=Meiji 14}}, and this Historic Site remains to this day. == built and area == The mound's area are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thin burial Decree|1=Thin Burial Edict|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622174}} of {{ill|646|en|646|WD=Q33285|lt=646}} restricted the construction of large Kofuns, yet high-Ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|en|Nakaoyama Kofun|WD=Q11363940|lt=Nakaoyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|en|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|WD=Q1039675|lt=Takamatsuzuka Kofun}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/040/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Emperor Tenmu] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/041/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Empress Jitō] - Imperial Household Agency {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|野口王墓|version=103308017|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:野口王墓]] {{Commons category|Noguchi Obo Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11645194}} [[Category:Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Empress Jitō]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Octagonal Kofun]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Noguchi Royal Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.468756|N|135.807825|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | built year = {{ill|687|en|687|WD=Q30303|lt=687}} (first year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō}}) | area = East-west 58m
Height 9m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} Historic Site "Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi" | excavations = | image = File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=28|coordinates=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|map_dot_label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|octagonal kofun|WD=Q11391751}} | commemorates = {{ill|Jitō|WD=Q232026}}; {{ill|Tenmu|WD=Q335181}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dating to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and the 41st Emperor {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Jitō}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in type, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound type is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with {{ill|Ashlar|en|Ashlar|WD=Q1122603|lt=Ashlar}}, reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquered coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Tenji}}'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Kofun|ja|御廟野古墳|1=Gobyō-no-Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyō-no Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyō-no-Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). However, the tomb was plundered in {{ill|1235|en|1235|WD=Q5425|lt=1235}}, during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient built === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of {{ill|687|en|687|WD=Q30303|lt=687}}, shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November {{ill|688|en|688|WD=Q30443|lt=688}}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|en|702|WD=Q23497|lt=702}} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|en|703|WD=Q23500|lt=703}}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|en|Fujiwara no Teika|WD=Q378425|lt=Fujiwara no Teika}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern built === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Restoration|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|en|1881|WD=Q7808|lt=Meiji 14}}, and this Historic Site remains to this day. == built and area == The mound's area are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thin burial Decree|1=Thin Burial Edict|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622174}} of {{ill|646|en|646|WD=Q33285|lt=646}} restricted the construction of large Kofuns, yet high-Ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|en|Nakaoyama Kofun|WD=Q11363940|lt=Nakaoyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|en|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|WD=Q1039675|lt=Takamatsuzuka Kofun}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/040/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Emperor Tenmu] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/041/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Empress Jitō] - Imperial Household Agency {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|野口王墓|version=103308017|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:野口王墓]] {{Commons category|Noguchi Obo Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11645194}} [[Category:Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Empress Jitō]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Octagonal Kofun]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern built === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Restoration|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|en|1881|WD=Q7808|lt=Meiji 14}}, and this Historic Site remains to this day. == built and area == The mound's area are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thin burial Decree|1=Thin Burial Edict|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622174}} of {{ill|646|en|646|WD=Q33285|lt=646}} restricted the construction of large Kofuns, yet high-Ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|en|Nakaoyama Kofun|WD=Q11363940|lt=Nakaoyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|en|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|WD=Q1039675|lt=Takamatsuzuka Kofun}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/040/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Emperor Tenmu] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/041/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Empress Jitō] - Imperial Household Agency {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|野口王墓|version=103308017|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:野口王墓]] {{Commons category|Noguchi Obo Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11645194}} [[Category:Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Empress Jitō]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Octagonal Kofun]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.comment: adding dummy category to make categories work
revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Noguchi Obo Kofun]][[ja:野口王墓]] [[co:commons:Category:Noguchi Obo Kofun]][[ja:野口王墓]] [[sn:commons:Category:Noguchi Obo Kofun]][[ja:野口王墓]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Noguchi Royal Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.468756|N|135.807825|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | built year = {{ill|687|en|687|WD=Q30303|lt=687}} (first year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō}}) | area = East-west 58m
Height 9m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} Historic Site "Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi" | excavations = | image = File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=28|coordinates=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|map_dot_label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|octagonal kofun|WD=Q11391751}} | commemorates = {{ill|Jitō|WD=Q232026}}; {{ill|Tenmu|WD=Q335181}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dating to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi'''" (檜隈大内陵), the mausoleum of the 40th Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and the 41st Emperor {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Jitō}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in type, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound type is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with {{ill|Ashlar|en|Ashlar|WD=Q1122603|lt=Ashlar}}, reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquered coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Tenji}}'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Kofun|ja|御廟野古墳|1=Gobyō-no-Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyō-no Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyō-no-Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). However, the tomb was plundered in {{ill|1235|en|1235|WD=Q5425|lt=1235}}, during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient built === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of {{ill|687|en|687|WD=Q30303|lt=687}}, shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November {{ill|688|en|688|WD=Q30443|lt=688}}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|en|702|WD=Q23497|lt=702}} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|en|703|WD=Q23500|lt=703}}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|en|Fujiwara no Teika|WD=Q378425|lt=Fujiwara no Teika}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern built === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Restoration|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|en|1881|WD=Q7808|lt=Meiji 14}}, and this Historic Site remains to this day. == built and area == The mound's area are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thin burial Decree|1=Thin Burial Edict|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622174}} of {{ill|646|en|646|WD=Q33285|lt=646}} restricted the construction of large Kofuns, yet high-Ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|en|Nakaoyama Kofun|WD=Q11363940|lt=Nakaoyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|en|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|WD=Q1039675|lt=Takamatsuzuka Kofun}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/040/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Emperor Tenmu] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/041/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Empress Jitō] - Imperial Household Agency {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|野口王墓|version=103308017|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:野口王墓]] {{Commons category|Noguchi Obo Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11645194}} [[Category:Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Empress Jitō]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Octagonal Kofun]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} refers to this tomb as "Ōuchi no Misasagi." == Overview == [[File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg|thumb|Noguchi Royal Tomb Kofun (2019)]] The mound is currently circular in type, with an east-west diameter of approximately 58 meters, a north-south diameter of 45 meters, and a height of 9 meters.[http://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20120518/t10015201481000.html "The tumulus is octagonal, confirmed over 50 years ago"] - NHK News (May 18, 2012). The original mound type is thought to have been octagonal, built in five tiers, with stone steps surrounding it. The burial chamber consists of two chambers constructed with {{ill|Ashlar|en|Ashlar|WD=Q1122603|lt=Ashlar|1=Ashlar}}, reportedly containing Emperor Tenmu's {{ill|Dry-lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquered coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}} and Empress Jitō's gilt bronze bone reliquary. This tomb is one of the few ancient imperial mausoleums considered authentic, as the buried individuals and historical accounts are consistent. Another comparable example is Emperor {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}}'s tomb ({{ill|Gobyō-no Kofun|ja|御廟野古墳|1=Gobyō-no-Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyō-no Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyō-no-Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). However, the tomb was plundered in {{ill|1235|en|1235|WD=Q5425|lt=1235|1=1235}}, during which most grave goods were stolen. Emperor Tenmu's coffin was desecrated, and his remains and white hair were reportedly scattered within the burial chamber. Empress Jitō's cremated remains, stored in a silver reliquary, were also stolen, and her ashes were discarded nearby. == Historical Mentions == === Ancient built === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} records that construction of the "Ōuchi no Misasagi" began in the tenth month of {{ill|687|en|687|WD=Q30303|lt=687|1=687}}, shortly after Emperor Tenmu's death. He was interred in November {{ill|688|en|688|WD=Q30443|lt=688|1=688}}. Empress Jitō passed away in {{ill|702|en|702|WD=Q23497|lt=702|1=702}} and was cremated at Asukaoka in December {{ill|703|en|703|WD=Q23500|lt=703|1=703}}, after which she was enshrined in the Ōuchi Mausoleum alongside Emperor Tenmu. === Middle Ages === The tomb suffered desecration in 1235, as documented in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|en|Fujiwara no Teika|WD=Q378425|lt=Fujiwara no Teika|1=Fujiwara no Teika}}'s diary, {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}. This incident led to the creation of the "{{ill|Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja|阿不幾乃山陵記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aofuki no Sanryōki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" record, describing the burial chamber and its treasures. === Modern built === During the {{ill|Bunkyū Restoration|ja|文久の修陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Restoration|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, initiated in 1862, the tomb was temporarily treated as Emperor Monmu's burial site. However, historical analysis reinstated it as Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō's mausoleum in {{ill|1881|en|1881|WD=Q7808|lt=Meiji 14|1=1881}}, and this Historic Site remains to this day. == built and area == The mound's area are as follows: * East-west diameter: 58 meters * Height: 9 meters The burial chambers feature octagonal designs and elaborate stonework, aligning with late Kofun period practices. == Thin burial Decree == The {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thin burial Decree|1=Thin Burial Edict|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622174}} of {{ill|646|en|646|WD=Q33285|lt=646|1=646}} restricted the construction of large Kofuns, yet high-Ranking officials and rulers continued the practice. The Noguchi Royal Tomb, along with other notable examples like {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|en|Nakaoyama Kofun|WD=Q11363940|lt=Nakaoyama Kofun|1=Nakaoyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|en|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|WD=Q1039675|lt=Takamatsuzuka Kofun|1=Takamatsuzuka Tomb}}, demonstrates this continuity. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/040/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Emperor Tenmu] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/041/index.html Hinokuma Ōuchi Mausoleum of Empress Jitō] - Imperial Household Agency {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|野口王墓|version=103308017|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:野口王墓]] {{Commons category|Noguchi Obo Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11645194}} [[Category:Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Empress Jitō]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Octagonal Kofun]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Height 9m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō|1=Empress Jitō}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} Historic Site "Hinokuma Ōuchi no Misasagi" | excavations = | image = File:NoguchiObo TenmuJito-ryo 201909b.jpg | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=28|coordinates=07.52|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=28.17|map_dot_label=Noguchi Royal Tomb|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|octagonal kofun|WD=Q11391751|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | commemorates = {{ill|Jitō|WD=Q232026|1=Empress Jitō}}; {{ill|Tenmu|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}} }} '''Noguchi Royal Tomb''' (野口王墓) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka, Nara|1=Asuka, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dating to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}.
